name: Hepatic failure
description: Hepatic failure — loss of the liver's synthetic and detoxifying function; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.

en.name: Hepatic failure
en.shortDescription: Hepatic failure — loss of the liver's synthetic and detoxifying function; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Hepatic failure is the loss of the liver's synthetic and detoxifying function, presenting with jaundice, coagulopathy and hepatic encephalopathy. It may be acute (in a previously normal liver) or occur on a background of chronic liver disease (acute-on-chronic). In intensive care it is both a reason for admission and a severe comorbidity. Hepatic failure is one of the chronic health items in the APACHE IV severity model.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *4245975 / 59927004 — Hepatic failure* with all descendants included, capturing acute, chronic and acute-on-chronic hepatic failure and related decompensation states.

## Mapping Notes

- Cirrhosis (structural scarring) and hepatic failure (functional loss) are distinct variables with their own sets; a patient may map to both.
- Hepatic encephalopathy is part of the hepatic-failure picture within this tree.
